title: Senior Data Analyst – Risk Services Insights
desc:
Sr Consult Bus Data Analysis - GA07AE
Sr Consultant Perf Analytics - GB07AE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Empower Strategy with Data. Drive Innovation with Insights.
Overview
The Risk Services Insights team is a group of curious, collaborative, and forward-thinking professionals who believe in the power of data to transform business. Our mission is to deliver timely, actionable insights that enable confident decision-making and fuel innovation across Risk Services. We value curiosity, collaboration, innovation, and impact—and we’re looking for someone who shares these values.
As a
Senior Data Analyst
, you will play a critical role in delivering the performance analytics that support operational effectiveness, resource optimization, and strategic decision-making across Risk Services. You will be hands-on from day one—building analytical tools, validating data, and translating complex performance metrics into compelling stories for senior leadership.
Responsibilities
Analyze service delivery performance and operational health metrics to uncover drivers of efficiency, quality, and customer impact.
Develop performance dashboards and KPI frameworks that enable leaders to monitor trends and evaluate business outcomes.
Analyze large datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and root causes; translate findings into clear, actionable recommendations.
Work through a robust backlog by independently prioritizing work, managing deadlines, and delivering high-quality outputs.
Lead requirements-gathering sessions with business partners; document user stories, acceptance criteria, and measurement definitions.
Drive conversations with stakeholders across the enterprise to clarify objectives, align on scope, and ensure successful delivery.
Leverage an understanding of data structures and architecture to troubleshoot data issues and partner with engineering teams.
Create and deliver polished, data-driven presentations tailored for senior leadership, translating complex analytical insights into business impact.
Qualifications
5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or performance measurement roles.
Expert-level proficiency with Snowflake, SQL, Tableau Desktop/Server, Tableau Prep, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Experience working with messy datasets, validating data, and performing complex analysis.
Familiarity with data structures, architecture concepts, and data engineering workflows.
Proven ability to analyze large datasets and convert insights into clear business recommendations.
Exceptional executive communication and data storytelling skills.
Experience with requirements gathering and structured documentation.
Demonstrated ability to prioritize work, communicate proactively, and keep stakeholders aligned.
Familiarity with Agile/Kanban methodologies and tools (e.g., Rally) is strongly preferred.
Experience in insurance, risk engineering, or related fields is preferred but not required.
Work Arrangement
This role offers a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement:
Candidates near Hartford, Chicago, Charlotte, or other major office locations are expected to work in-office 3 days per week (Tuesday–Thursday).
Remote candidates will work virtually, with occasional travel as business needs arise.
Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
Why Join Us?
If you’re passionate about data analytics, performance measurement, and shaping the future of Risk Engineering, this is your opportunity to make an impact, gain enterprise visibility, and grow your career in a dynamic, innovation-driven environment. Apply today and join a team where your insights drive action.

desc:
Sr Consultant Risk Engineering - KR08DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Do you have a passion for identifying risks? Are you a practical thinker who’s driven to help others? This is your opportunity to give those passions and talents new purpose by applying them to meaningful work at The Hartford.
As a Risk Engineering Consultant at The Hartford, your primary role will be to protect the future of and ensure profitability for The Hartford, its business partners and agents through timely delivery of industry leading safety and risk management solutions.
This role will have a Remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
WORK ARRANGEMENTS:
This role will have a Remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise. The territory will primarily include New York, New York & New Jersey.
This position can be hired at various levels depending on background and experience.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Prepare for and conduct presentations to serviced accounts
• Based on knowledge of the insured’s business activities and needs, make recommendations that will assist the insured’s management to correct and prevent the potential for future financial loss
• Work with regional office Risk Engineering Lead/Primary Mentor and assist in all levels of training for new and experienced hires. This can include conducting report and file reviews, coaching, etc.
• Active participation in special projects that could be technical in nature (i.e.) Biotech, Technology, Industry Expertise, etc. and work to become a Subject Matter Expert in one technical discipline
• Active participation in the RMS process - meeting with insured’s, making presentations, working with claims and underwriting, etc.
• Work with Risk Engineering Manager on special projects and developing reports to support Regional Office objectives
QUALIFICATIONS:
• High school diploma required; a BA/BS degree is required with coursework focused on Occupational Safety and Health Studies, Fire Sciences, Pure Sciences, Engineering or related subjects is strongly preferred
• A minimum of 5 years of industry experience in a safety related role; property and casualty insurance experience highly desired
• Ability to achieve or maintain 1 professional safety certification/designation (i.e.) CSP, CIH, CPCU, CFPS, ARM, etc.
• Must have the knowledge and skill to handle all Survey and Service work assignments for all lines of business
• Strong consultative skills. Must work at an advanced skill level when providing service. This would include the demonstration of operational loss analysis reports and accident investigations used to direct services that would eliminate or control conditions that could lead to potential losses
• Must have the ability to complete detailed service plans; provide consultative recommendations in business letters; provide safety training to address losses (i.e.) Ergonomics, Machine Safeguarding, Industrial Hygiene; and have the ability to use the tools and products to provide the services necessary.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. desc:

Senior Executive Underwriter - UW07CC
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
As an Executive Underwriter in National Accounts at The Hartford, you will be responsible for the casualty lines of National Accounts (typically accounts with more than 500 employees) which are written utilizing loss sensitive plans, such as large deductibles and self-insured retentions. You will underwrite and manage a book of business as well as working with brokers and clients on servicing that book, driving new business opportunities, and developing relationships with assigned brokers located throughout the territory that are assigned to.
Responsibilities
Creatively use underwriting tools, techniques, and program structures to write profitable business while maintaining disciplined adherence to underwriting and service standards.
Develop and maintain strong relationships with national and large regional brokers assigned to you.
Successfully execute your territorial business plan to achieve financial objectives.
Achieve annual account retention objectives through effective account stewardship practices.
Achieve annual growth objectives for your territory by building a robust pipeline of viable opportunities and displaying strong sales behaviors to successfully acquire new accounts.
Develop and maintain strong relationships with key internal and external partners to ensure that your accounts receive a high level of service.
Maintain satisfactory underwriting quality control standards.
Qualifications
At least 5 + years of casualty lines underwriting experience with a preference towards building and selling loss-sensitive programs.
Able to coordinate service team to meet day to day demands.
Able to articulate the benefits of strong loss prevention programs, proactive claims management and effective RMIS capabilities. Strong organizational and workflow management skills. Ability to work independently.
Able to seek out and attract new business opportunities within a defined territory.
Proven track record of strong sales practices and excellent presentation skills to brokers and clients.
College degree in finance, accounting, economics or insurance is strongly preferred.
Valid driver's license as a company pool car may be provided.
Ability to travel to be in front of clients and producers.
Location
This role will have a Hybrid work arrangement, with the expectation of working in the Brea, CA office three days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. title: Director of Data Engineering, Data Science Team
desc:
Dir Data Engineering - GE06AE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford is seeking a Director of Data Engineering within Global Specialty Data Science & Analytics  to lead a team of Data Engineers to design, develop, and implement modern and sustainable data assets to fuel machine learning and artificial intelligence solutions, as well as business intelligence assets and other analytic work across a wide range of strategic initiatives.
As a Director of Data Engineering, you will lead a team through the entire software development lifecycle process in support of continuous data delivery, while growing your knowledge of emerging technologies. We use the latest data technologies, software engineering practices, AIOPs, Agile delivery frameworks, and are passionate about building well-architected and innovative solutions that drive business value. This cutting edge and forward focused organization presents the opportunity for collaboration, self-organization within the team, and visibility as we focus on continuous business data delivery.
This role will have a Hybrid work arrangement, with the expectation of working in an office location (Charlotte NC, Chicago IL, Columbus OH or Hartford CT) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Responsibilities:
Actively lead and develop a team of data engineers to deliver and maintain reusable and sustainable data assets and production pipelines that assist the functional business units in meeting their strategic objectives
Collaborate with Data Scientists and Product Owners to advise approaches to business opportunities
Develop the design and vision for data pipelines and testing frameworks, both batch and real-time to meet business needs while balancing maintainability, reliability, security, and scalability.
Provide guidance on and independently navigate data warehouses, understand data architectures, and join disparate data sources to ensure quality and appropriateness of data solutions.
Lead the use and development of GitHub best practices for version control, documentation, and code collaboration throughout the data science lifecycle and ensure solutions align with best practices
Contribute to and execute a multi-year roadmap to build and remediate enterprise-grade data assets leveraging cloud-based target state technology and architecture
Drive disciplined innovation by balancing a relentless focus on delivering results and customer adoption with out-of-the-box thinking and a continuous improvement mindset
Coordinate activities with cross-functional IT unit stakeholders (e.g., database, operations, telecommunications, technical support, etc.)
Qualifications:
8+ years of relevant experience recommended
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, IT, MIS, or a related discipline
Experience in managing Data Engineering teams in an Agile environment
Experience designing and delivering scalable data engineering solutions that integrate GenAI capabilities and agentic AI systems
Expertise in Python and SQL
Expertise in ingesting data from a variety of structures including relational databases, Hadoop/Spark, cloud data sources, XML, JSON
Expertise in ETL concerning metadata management and data validation
Expertise in Unix and Git
Expertise in Automation tools (Autosys, Cron, Airflow, etc.)
Experience with AWS Services (i.e. S3, EMR, etc.) or GCP
Experience with Cloud data warehouses, automation, and data pipelines (i.e. Snowflake, Redshift) a plus
Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical teams
Able to translate complex technical topics into business solutions and strategies as well as turn business requirements into a technical solution
Experience with leading project execution and driving change to core business processes through the innovative use of quantitative techniques
Candidates must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship.
The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.

title: Construction Defect - Claim Specialist
desc:
Specialist Claims - CH07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
This is a visible and important role within our Complex Claims Unit (CCU) Hartford Global Specialty (HGS) Claims Division. As a Claims Specialist, you will be responsible for handling a caseload of higher complexity, higher exposure, construction defect and construction related property damage claims from inception to final disposition.  These claims will involve both primary and excess coverages and often involve complex fact patterns requiring analysis of contracts between parties to determine indemnity and contribution obligations and risk transfer opportunities. As these claims are often in litigation, experience handling litigated matters and managing defense counsel is required, as is prior experience handling construction defect and property damage claims. Excess claims handling experience is recommended, but not required.
Responsibilities include, but are not limited, to:
Managing a caseload of litigated and non-litigated construction defect claims under commercial general liability policies.
Conducting investigations and analyzing and evaluating the information learned.
Making coverage determinations and communicating written position(s) to insureds and other required parties.
Within prescribed authority levels, setting appropriate expense and indemnity reserves and monitoring on a regular basis for any needed adjustment.
Presenting cases to management for expense or indemnity reserve authority above established authority levels.
Developing and implementing resolution strategies to achieve high quality outcomes.
Proactively managing litigation and counsel, inclusive of litigation planning and execution, budgeting and bill review.
Attending trials and mediations as necessary.
Contributing to broader claim and enterprise goals by participating in audits, projects and product development initiatives.
Preparing comprehensive reports and delivering presentations to senior claim leadership on case developments, policy issues, industry trends, etc.
Working with business partners to evaluate and address claim trends and developments.
Addressing inquiries from agents and policyholders and providing superior customer service.
Position Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree preferred, law degree a plus or commensurate experience
Minimum of seven years handling complex litigated construction defect and property damage matters
Familiarity with owner and contractor controlled insurance policies and programs as well as wrap policies, is a plus
High level of discipline, results-oriented and able to focus on bottom line results
Superior analytical ability and organizational skills
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Excellent strategic thinking ability and execution skills
Excellent negotiation and advanced technical claim handling skills, including knowledge of coverage and tort laws
Full command of damages issues relative to high value construction defect and property damage claims
Strong ability to analyze coverage and liability issues, manage time limit demands and assess extra contractual exposures and other issues of complexity
Ability to communicate thoughts clearly and concisely, and to influence and persuade others
Superior interpersonal skills
Ability to exceed expectations and influence others to do the same
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations (Hartford, CT, San Antonio, TX, Lake Mary, FL, Phoenix, AZ, Naperville, IL, Alpharetta, GA) will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$107,600 - $161,400
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

title: Construction Defect - Claim Specialist
desc:
Specialist Claims - CH07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
This is a visible and important role within our Complex Claims Unit (CCU) Hartford Global Specialty (HGS) Claims Division. As a Claims Specialist, you will be responsible for handling a caseload of higher complexity, higher exposure, construction defect and construction related property damage claims from inception to final disposition.  These claims will involve both primary and excess coverages and often involve complex fact patterns requiring analysis of contracts between parties to determine indemnity and contribution obligations and risk transfer opportunities. As these claims are often in litigation, experience handling litigated matters and managing defense counsel is required, as is prior experience handling construction defect and property damage claims. Excess claims handling experience is recommended, but not required.
Responsibilities include, but are not limited, to:
Managing a caseload of litigated and non-litigated construction defect claims under commercial general liability policies.
Conducting investigations and analyzing and evaluating the information learned.
Making coverage determinations and communicating written position(s) to insureds and other required parties.
Within prescribed authority levels, setting appropriate expense and indemnity reserves and monitoring on a regular basis for any needed adjustment.
Presenting cases to management for expense or indemnity reserve authority above established authority levels.
Developing and implementing resolution strategies to achieve high quality outcomes.
Proactively managing litigation and counsel, inclusive of litigation planning and execution, budgeting and bill review.
Attending trials and mediations as necessary.
Contributing to broader claim and enterprise goals by participating in audits, projects and product development initiatives.
Preparing comprehensive reports and delivering presentations to senior claim leadership on case developments, policy issues, industry trends, etc.
Working with business partners to evaluate and address claim trends and developments.
Addressing inquiries from agents and policyholders and providing superior customer service.
Position Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree preferred, law degree a plus or commensurate experience
Minimum of seven years handling complex litigated construction defect and property damage matters
Familiarity with owner and contractor controlled insurance policies and programs as well as wrap policies, is a plus
High level of discipline, results-oriented and able to focus on bottom line results
Superior analytical ability and organizational skills
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Excellent strategic thinking ability and execution skills
Excellent negotiation and advanced technical claim handling skills, including knowledge of coverage and tort laws
Full command of damages issues relative to high value construction defect and property damage claims
Strong ability to analyze coverage and liability issues, manage time limit demands and assess extra contractual exposures and other issues of complexity
Ability to communicate thoughts clearly and concisely, and to influence and persuade others
Superior interpersonal skills
Ability to exceed expectations and influence others to do the same
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations (Hartford, CT, San Antonio, TX, Lake Mary, FL, Phoenix, AZ, Naperville, IL, Alpharetta, GA) will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$107,600 - $161,400
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual

title: Claims Consultant EPL
desc:
Claims Consultant FL - CV07GE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford Global Specialty - Financial Lines Employment Practices Liability (“EPL”) Claims Group currently has an open Claim Consultant. As an EPL Claims Professional, you will be responsible for handling a caseload of claims-made, EPL claims, from inception to final disposition. As these claims are often duty-to-defend and in litigation, experience handling litigated EPL cases through resolution and managing defense counsel is required.
Responsibilities include all aspects of claim file management from assignment to conclusion including:
Conducting investigations and analyzing and evaluating the information learned
Appropriately and accurately analyzing and determining coverage, liability and damages based upon the facts of each claim
Communicating written position(s) to insureds and other required parties on coverage, liability, damages and other issues
Setting appropriate expense and indemnity reserves and monitoring on a regular basis for any needed adjustments
Presenting cases to management for expense and/or indemnity reserve authority above established authority levels
Developing and implementing resolution strategies through negotiations to achieve high quality outcomes
Proactively managing litigation and counsel, inclusive of litigation planning and execution, budgeting and bill review
Understanding and analyzing potential extra-contractual liability as needed
Attending trials and mediations as necessary
Contributing to broader claim and enterprise goals by participating in audits, projects, training and product development initiatives
Preparing comprehensive reports and delivering presentations to senior claim leadership on case developments, policy issues, industry trends, etc.
Providing support and working collaboratively with business partners to evaluate and address claim trends and developments
Address inquiries from agents and policyholders, providing superior customer service
Provide key training and marketing support to Underwriters
Willingness to attend and present at various industry events each year on risk management and claims topics
Willingness to organize and present webinars and in-house risk management seminars for high profile insureds
Qualifications:
Juris Doctorate degree strongly preferred, College Degree Required
Minimum of 7 years handling litigated coverage and liability cases and/or claims
Candidate should be disciplined, independently motivated, results-oriented and able to focus on bottom line results
Candidates are required to have knowledge and experience handling all aspects of EPL claims
Candidates should demonstrate the following:
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Excellent strategic thinking ability and execution skills
Excellent negotiation and advanced technical claim handling skills, including knowledge of insurance coverage issues
Superior time, task prioritization and desk management skills.
An ability to communicate thoughts clearly and concisely, and to influence and persuade others
Ability to work with only moderate amount of supervision and employ a “team player” attitude.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations (Hartford, CT, San Antonio, TX, Lake Mary, FL, Phoenix, AZ; Alpharetta GA, Naperville, IL) will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$122,400 - $183,600

title: Senior Staff Attorney - Insurance Defense
desc:
Sr Staff Attorney - LM07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford currently has an in-house opportunity for a Senior Staff Attorney to litigate cases focused in the greater Los Angeles Area and surrounding regions. This Remote position is an ideal fit for an experienced Attorney with significant trial and litigation experience in Premises Liability, Products Liability, Construction Site accidents and Commercial/Personal Automobile accidents. The Senior Staff Attorney will strive to deliver the best possible result in pending litigation. In this position the attorney reports to the Staff Legal Managing Attorney or Assistant Managing Attorney.
The ideal candidate is an experienced Attorney with significant knowledge of Southern California courts, considerable trial experience, and demonstrates the ability to independently handle cases from inception through trial.
RESPONSBILITIES:
• Handle complex and high exposure litigation.
• Analyze intricate substantive and procedural legal and factual issues,
conduct extensive, well-reasoned legal research, independently develop
and present defense strategies on behalf of clients.
• Prepare complex pleadings, written discovery, depositions, motions and
briefs in support of defense strategies without supervision due to level
of experience and expertise.
• Provide effective and timely communications, information, legal advice
and other services to clients and Claims customers on legal and factual
issues in a technology driven environment.
• Communicate with the court, witnesses, opposing counsel and co-coun
sel in a manner consistent with established office procedures.
• Provide for the prompt, efficient and effective disposition of assigned
cases.
• Independently prepare and present witnesses and evidence at trials, jud
icial and administrative hearings and alternative dispute resolutions.
• Research, draft, file and argue motions on behalf of clients
• Provide damage and liability opinions to clients and Claims customers.
QUALIFICATIONS:
• Juris Doctorate (JD) from accredited law school and license to practice
in California and local federal courts.
• Knowledge of California courts, particularly those in Southern
California.
• Member in good standing of applicable Bar Association.
• 7+ years of legal experience.
• Documented first chair jury trial/appellate expertise.
• Strong legal research and writing skills.
• Excellent organizational skills and ability to prioritize duties and time
are a must.
• Strong communication, computer and technological skills and the abil
ity to quickly leverage new software and technologies as required.
• Demonstrated expertise and familiarity in the handling of claim cases
and litigation.
WHAT ELSE CAN YOU TELL ME?
This position is a remote work opportunity however; our selected candidate may travel periodically within the Southern California territory with the focus being;  travel in and around the Greater Los Angels and surrounding areas.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$132,400 - $198,600
Equal Opportunity

title: Senior Staff Attorney Insurance Defense
desc:
Sr Staff Attorney - LM07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
This posting is intended to build a pipeline of qualified candidates for anticipated future openings. While there is no immediate vacancy we are actively identifying talent for upcoming needs.
The Hartford currently has an in-house REMOTE opportunity for a Senior Staff Attorney to litigate cases primarily in Queens, Nassau and Suffolk Counties. This position is an ideal fit for an experienced Attorney with significant trial and litigation experience in Labor Law/Construction site accidents, Premises Liability, Products Liability as well as Commercial and Personal Automobile accidents. The Senior Staff Attorney will work diligently to deliver the best possible result in pending litigation. In this position the attorney reports to the Staff Legal Managing Attorney or Assistant Managing Attorney. The ideal candidate is an experienced Attorney with demonstrated trial experience and the ability to handle cases independently from inception through trial.
RESPONSBILITIES:
• Handle complex and high exposure litigation
• Analyze intricate substantive and procedural legal and factual issues, conduct extensive, well-reasoned legal research, independently develop and present defense strategies on behalf of a variety of clients
• Prepare complex pleadings, written discovery, depositions, motions and briefs in support of defense strategies with minimal supervision because of level of experience and expertise
• Provide effective and timely communications, information, legal advice and other services to clients and claims partners on legal and factual issues in a technology driven environment
• Communicate respectfully with the Court, clients, claims partners, witnesses, opposing counsel and co-counsel in a manner consistent with established office procedures and professional standards.
• Provide for the prompt, efficient and effective disposition of assigned cases
• Independently prepare and present witnesses and evidence at trials, judicial and administrative hearings and alternative dispute resolutions
• Research, draft, file and argue appellate briefs in reviewing courts on behalf of clients

• Strong legal research and writing skills
• Excellent organizational skills and ability to prioritize duties and time are a must
• Strong communication, computer and technological skills and the ability to quickly leverage new A.I. tools
• Demonstrated expertise and familiarity in the handling of claim cases and litigation

title: Claims Consultant EPL
desc:
Claims Consultant FL - CV07GE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford Global Specialty - Financial Lines Employment Practices Liability (“EPL”) Claims Group currently has an open Claim Consultant. As an EPL Claims Professional, you will be responsible for handling a caseload of claims-made, EPL claims, from inception to final disposition. As these claims are often duty-to-defend and in litigation, experience handling litigated EPL cases through resolution and managing defense counsel is required.
Responsibilities include all aspects of claim file management from assignment to conclusion including:
Conducting investigations and analyzing and evaluating the information learned
Appropriately and accurately analyzing and determining coverage, liability and damages based upon the facts of each claim
Communicating written position(s) to insureds and other required parties on coverage, liability, damages and other issues
Setting appropriate expense and indemnity reserves and monitoring on a regular basis for any needed adjustments
Presenting cases to management for expense and/or indemnity reserve authority above established authority levels
Developing and implementing resolution strategies through negotiations to achieve high quality outcomes
Proactively managing litigation and counsel, inclusive of litigation planning and execution, budgeting and bill review
Understanding and analyzing potential extra-contractual liability as needed
Attending trials and mediations as necessary
Contributing to broader claim and enterprise goals by participating in audits, projects, training and product development initiatives
Preparing comprehensive reports and delivering presentations to senior claim leadership on case developments, policy issues, industry trends, etc.
Providing support and working collaboratively with business partners to evaluate and address claim trends and developments
Address inquiries from agents and policyholders, providing superior customer service
Provide key training and marketing support to Underwriters
Willingness to attend and present at various industry events each year on risk management and claims topics
Willingness to organize and present webinars and in-house risk management seminars for high profile insureds
Qualifications:
Juris Doctorate degree strongly preferred, College Degree Required
Minimum of 7 years handling litigated coverage and liability cases and/or claims
Candidate should be disciplined, independently motivated, results-oriented and able to focus on bottom line results
Candidates are required to have knowledge and experience handling all aspects of EPL claims
Candidates should demonstrate the following:
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Excellent strategic thinking ability and execution skills
Excellent negotiation and advanced technical claim handling skills, including knowledge of insurance coverage issues
Superior time, task prioritization and desk management skills.
An ability to communicate thoughts clearly and concisely, and to influence and persuade others
Ability to work with only moderate amount of supervision and employ a “team player” attitude.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations (Hartford, CT, San Antonio, TX, Lake Mary, FL, Phoenix, AZ; Alpharetta GA, Naperville, IL) will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$122,400 - $183,600
Equal Opportunity

title: Senior Cloud Engineer - Infrastructure as Code
desc:
Sr Cloud Engineer - IE07NE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Position Overview
We are seeking a highly capable Cloud Database Engineer to design, automate and support cloud-based database solutions. The role requires the use of Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) tools like Terraform and AWS’s Cloud Formation. This role blends cloud engineering, database administration, and automation skills to ensure that our database environments are secure, scalable and operationally efficient.
The successful candidate will work across Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud Products, enterprise logging services, automation platforms, and code management tools.
This role will have a Hybrid work schedule, with the expectation of working in an office (Columbus, OH, Chicago, IL, Hartford, CT or Charlotte, NC) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Key Responsibilities
Design and implement cloud-based database architectures that meet application requirements and organizational goals.
Collaborate with application teams to architect scalable, secure, and high-performing cloud database solutions that align with enterprise standards.
Partner with the Cloud Engineering and Site Reliability Engineers to integrate database CI/CD pipelines into their application deployments. Including Jenkins and Ansible.
Have in-depth knowledge of designing and building automation for database provisioning and repetitive tasks using infrastructure as code tools, including AWS’s CloudFormation and Terraform.
Develop and maintain disaster recovery and high availability strategies.
Proficient at preparing deployment and standard operational procedure manuals.
Manage source control, branching strategies, and CI/CD workflows using GitHub
Automate the monitoring and alerting of database performance and availability.
Evaluate new product releases and make recommendations on implementation of newest versions.
Integrate database workflows with ServiceNow for change and incident management.
Provide second-level support for troubleshooting production incidents.
Required Skills & Experience
>10 years of professional experience managing relational and NoSQL databases in a cloud environment (AWS and GCP).
Hands-on experience with Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform and CloudFormation
Experience with GitHub for version control and workflow automation
Familiarity with Jenkins pipelines, Ansible automation and Service Now.
Solid understanding of networking concepts, VPCs, subnets, and security best practices as they relate to database deployments.
Strong organizational and communication skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
A passion for engineering excellence and continuous improvement.
Effective communication and collaboration skills to work across cross-functional teams. Excellent critical thinking skills
Bachelor’s degree in computer science or related discipline
Strong documentation skills in creating and maintaining operational policies and procedures.
A desire for continuous learning
Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$137,200 - $205,800

title: Intern, Enterprise Risk Management
desc:
Student Intern - HHSIAN
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) organization helps the firm understand, manage and anticipate risk. Our goal is to proactively provide an independent assessment of the firm’s risk while supporting the business segments’ pursuit of profitable growth. ERM is responsible for traditional risks such as Financial & Market Risk, Insurance Risk, and Operational Risk, as well as understanding new and emerging risks such as Cyber Security and AI.
What’s in it for you?
Joining The Hartford’s ERM Summer Internship Program is a great way to launch your career, enhance your business acumen and gain insight into the risk management field. Summer interns are placed into meaningful roles that have an immediate impact on the business. Over the course of ten weeks, you will:
Develop your knowledge of risk management and required corporate skills during a weekly training series
Attend multiple collaborative events with ERM teammates and leadership
Apply your education and unique skills to a summer-long project culminating in a Capstone presentation to senior management
Gain career accelerating skills through on-the-job learning, exposure to ERM and Hartford leadership, and 1-on-1 mentoring
Successful interns may be offered full-time employment into The Hartford’s ERM organization upon graduation.
What is The Hartford looking for?
Students expecting to graduate in January 2026 or May of 2027 with a Bachelor’s degree and a GPA of 3.2 or higher
Desired majors include, but are not limited to Business, Finance, IT, Math, Economics, Geoscience, Statistics, Risk Management & Insurance, or Engineering
Interest in risk management
Analytical and critical thinking/problem solving skills
Strong communication and leadership skills
Desire to work in a
collaborative/team-based
environment

title: Senior Underwriter
desc:
INTL Senior Underwriter I - UW07VV
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the future.
Purpose of the Job
To underwrite and develop a profitable International Upstream book.
To offer outstanding service to our clients.
Job Responsibilities
Use authority granted in Underwriting Authority Statement to execute on the Upstream Energy business plan and assist in producing future business plans.
Identify, develop, and secure new business opportunities within key distribution channels across the London Market and global broker networks, strengthening The Hartford’s presence in the Upstream Energy segment.
Contribute to underwriting activities related to the renewal portfolio of Upstream Energy business, ensuring disciplined risk selection and portfolio profitability.
Participate in the development and implementation of the Upstream Energy marketing strategy, supporting growth objectives in the London Market and internationally.
Utilise all available underwriting tools, including technical rating models, exposure assessments, engineering reports, and market benchmarking to support risk-based decision-making.
Assist in further development of systems for the monitoring and reporting of aggregate exposures and help further develop and maintain technical pricing models.
Support the Head of Energy in growing Upstream Energy portfolio, contributing to underwriting governance and business planning.
Assist with travel and marketing initiatives aimed at promoting The Hartford brand to brokers, clients, and other industry partners within the Upstream Energy space
Ensure compliance with The Hartford’s internal best practices as well as Lloyd’s, regulatory, and local market requirements relevant to Energy underwriting.
Keep abreast of development of claims within the Upstream Energy book and to liaise with the claims team as necessary to ensure appropriate settlement of these claims.
Contribute to monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting of underwriting performance and portfolio analytics as required.
Help to mentor and educate other team members as required.
Participate as required in various committees, including underwriting, delegated underwriting, operations and risk.
Qualifications and Capabilities
ACII qualified or strong progression thereto, &/or degree level education
Knowledge of general insurance and its underlying principles
Willingness to continue to develop knowledge of Insurance and related issues through form and informal learning
Ability to develop and sustain relationships with internal and external customers
Ability to prioritise and organise workload
Attention to detail
Team player with ability to work on a stand-alone basis
Flexible
Good communication skills
Experience of identifying and implementing better processes
Experience
Demonstrable and strong underwriting or broking experience
Ability to demonstrate technical capability, experience and understanding of the Upstream Energy business
Strong market facing/broker development skills, including writing and leading new business
Interpersonal skills and ability to operate independently in a small team
This role will be based in London, UK and you are required to have the necessary right to work details prior to application.

title: Senior Staff Attorney - Insurance Defense
desc:
Sr Staff Attorney - LM07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford currently has an in-house opportunity for a Senior Staff Attorney to litigate cases focused in the greater Los Angeles Area and surrounding regions. This Remote position is an ideal fit for an experienced Attorney with significant trial and litigation experience in Premises Liability, Products Liability, Construction Site accidents and Commercial/Personal Automobile accidents. The Senior Staff Attorney will strive to deliver the best possible result in pending litigation. In this position the attorney reports to the Staff Legal Managing Attorney or Assistant Managing Attorney.
The ideal candidate is an experienced Attorney with significant knowledge of Southern California courts, considerable trial experience, and demonstrates the ability to independently handle cases from inception through trial.
RESPONSBILITIES:
• Handle complex and high exposure litigation.
• Analyze intricate substantive and procedural legal and factual issues,
conduct extensive, well-reasoned legal research, independently develop
and present defense strategies on behalf of clients.
• Prepare complex pleadings, written discovery, depositions, motions and
briefs in support of defense strategies without supervision due to level
of experience and expertise.
• Provide effective and timely communications, information, legal advice
and other services to clients and Claims customers on legal and factual
issues in a technology driven environment.
• Communicate with the court, witnesses, opposing counsel and co-coun
sel in a manner consistent with established office procedures.
• Provide for the prompt, efficient and effective disposition of assigned
cases.
• Independently prepare and present witnesses and evidence at trials, jud
icial and administrative hearings and alternative dispute resolutions.
• Research, draft, file and argue motions on behalf of clients
• Provide damage and liability opinions to clients and Claims customers.
QUALIFICATIONS:
• Juris Doctorate (JD) from accredited law school and license to practice
in California and local federal courts.
• Knowledge of California courts, particularly those in Southern
California.
• Member in good standing of applicable Bar Association.
• 7+ years of legal experience.
• Documented first chair jury trial/appellate expertise.
• Strong legal research and writing skills.
• Excellent organizational skills and ability to prioritize duties and time
are a must.
• Strong communication, computer and technological skills and the abil
ity to quickly leverage new software and technologies as required.
• Demonstrated expertise and familiarity in the handling of claim cases
and litigation.
WHAT ELSE CAN YOU TELL ME?
This position is a remote work opportunity however; our selected candidate may travel periodically within the Southern California territory with the focus being;  travel in and around the Greater Los Angels and surrounding areas.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Collaborate with application teams to architect scalable, secure, and high-performing cloud database solutions that align with enterprise standards.
Partner with the Cloud Engineering and Site Reliability Engineers to integrate database CI/CD pipelines into their application deployments. Including Jenkins and Ansible.
Have in-depth knowledge of designing and building automation for database provisioning and repetitive tasks using infrastructure as code tools, including AWS’s CloudFormation and Terraform.
Develop and maintain disaster recovery and high availability strategies.
Proficient at preparing deployment and standard operational procedure manuals.
Manage source control, branching strategies, and CI/CD workflows using GitHub
Automate the monitoring and alerting of database performance and availability.
Evaluate new product releases and make recommendations on implementation of newest versions.
Integrate database workflows with ServiceNow for change and incident management.
Provide second-level support for troubleshooting production incidents.
Required Skills & Experience
>10 years of professional experience managing relational and NoSQL databases in a cloud environment (AWS and GCP).
Hands-on experience with Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform and CloudFormation
Experience with GitHub for version control and workflow automation
Familiarity with Jenkins pipelines, Ansible automation and Service Now.
Solid understanding of networking concepts, VPCs, subnets, and security best practices as they relate to database deployments.
Strong organizational and communication skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities.
A passion for engineering excellence and continuous improvement.
Effective communication and collaboration skills to work across cross-functional teams. Excellent critical thinking skills
Bachelor’s degree in computer science or related discipline
Strong documentation skills in creating and maintaining operational policies and procedures.
A desire for continuous learning
Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. $137,200 - $205,800

title: Claims Consultant EPL
desc:
Claims Consultant FL - CV07GE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford Global Specialty - Financial Lines Employment Practices Liability (“EPL”) Claims Group currently has an open Claim Consultant. As an EPL Claims Professional, you will be responsible for handling a caseload of claims-made, EPL claims, from inception to final disposition. As these claims are often duty-to-defend and in litigation, experience handling litigated EPL cases through resolution and managing defense counsel is required.
Responsibilities include all aspects of claim file management from assignment to conclusion including:
Conducting investigations and analyzing and evaluating the information learned
Appropriately and accurately analyzing and determining coverage, liability and damages based upon the facts of each claim
Communicating written position(s) to insureds and other required parties on coverage, liability, damages and other issues
Setting appropriate expense and indemnity reserves and monitoring on a regular basis for any needed adjustments
Presenting cases to management for expense and/or indemnity reserve authority above established authority levels
Developing and implementing resolution strategies through negotiations to achieve high quality outcomes
Proactively managing litigation and counsel, inclusive of litigation planning and execution, budgeting and bill review
Understanding and analyzing potential extra-contractual liability as needed
Attending trials and mediations as necessary
Contributing to broader claim and enterprise goals by participating in audits, projects, training and product development initiatives
Preparing comprehensive reports and delivering presentations to senior claim leadership on case developments, policy issues, industry trends, etc.
Providing support and working collaboratively with business partners to evaluate and address claim trends and developments
Address inquiries from agents and policyholders, providing superior customer service
Provide key training and marketing support to Underwriters
Willingness to attend and present at various industry events each year on risk management and claims topics
Willingness to organize and present webinars and in-house risk management seminars for high profile insureds
Qualifications:
Juris Doctorate degree strongly preferred, College Degree Required
Minimum of 7 years handling litigated coverage and liability cases and/or claims
Candidate should be disciplined, independently motivated, results-oriented and able to focus on bottom line results
Candidates are required to have knowledge and experience handling all aspects of EPL claims
Candidates should demonstrate the following:
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Excellent strategic thinking ability and execution skills
Excellent negotiation and advanced technical claim handling skills, including knowledge of insurance coverage issues
Superior time, task prioritization and desk management skills.
An ability to communicate thoughts clearly and concisely, and to influence and persuade others
Ability to work with only moderate amount of supervision and employ a “team player” attitude.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations (Hartford, CT, San Antonio, TX, Lake Mary, FL, Phoenix, AZ; Alpharetta GA, Naperville, IL) will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.

title: Account Engineer Large Property-Risk Engineering (Northeast Region)
desc:
Exec Consultant Risk Eng - KR07HE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford is continuing to expand its Risk Engineering Property Team and is seeking an Account Engineer with Highly Protected Risk (HPR) skills and experience. This role will support the expansion and growth of the Large Property Unit. The position will report into the Managing Director for Large Property, Risk Engineering Organization.
It will have accountability specific to the deployment of Underwriting and Risk Engineering practices for the Property Line of Business, with support to the Large Property Unit.
This role must reside in the Northeast and can have a Hybrid or Remote work schedule.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations will have the expectation of working in an office periodically. Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work schedule, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise. Some business travel is required.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide Risk Engineering expertise at the Account Level, support and assistance to The Hartford and its customers, to assist Large Property Underwriting in risk evaluation of accounts.
Develop and maintain professional working relationships with Underwriting, Claims, Other HIG Business Partners, Clients and Brokers.
Support independently large clients or clients with complex business.
Complete desk top reviews and risk assessments for underwriting evaluating and summarizing exposures and controls.
Work with underwriting, the client and the broker where appropriate to develop customized Risk Engineering programs including locations to be surveyed (or included as part of service process)
Develop and implement Account Servicing instructions to guide Risk Engineering services being provided to assigned accounts, and develop year end account summaries for underwriting and the client as required by the different lines of business.
Review, analyze and prioritize reports, recommendations, and correspondence received from other Property Specialists, Risk Engineering Field Consultants, clients, brokers, etc.
QUALIFICATIONS:
BA/BS degree in Engineering or Fire Sciences is preferred. Other disciplines will be considered in conjunction with extensive technical expertise or experience in the HPR insurance industry.
Experience in insurance or related field is required with a minimum of 7+ years related experience.
Certification/designation(s) (i.e.) CFPS, PE, CSP, ARM, etc. (at least one is preferred, but not required)
Highly Protected Risk (HPR) skills and experience required.
Superior analytical and problem solving skills. Ability to work in a fast changing, dynamic environment.
The ability to work on multiple tasks and assignments.
Results orientated and high level of accountability. Motivated, self-starter.
Works independently with minimal oversight.

title: Risk Engineer (All Levels & Specialty Areas)
desc:
Sr Consultant Risk Engineering - KR08DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
At The Hartford, we are seeking top talent to meet our future needs - forward-thinking Risk Engineers who blend deep technical expertise with a passion for innovation, customer centricity, and digital fluency. In this risk mitigation role, you’ll leverage AI-powered tools, data-driven insights, and emerging technologies to deliver proactive, tailored risk solutions that help clients protect their assets and achieve their goals.
As a Risk Engineer, you’ll conduct survey work while working as a strategic advisor—collaborating across disciplines, driving digital transformation, and continuously learning to stay ahead of industry trends. You’ll use empathy and active listening to understand client priorities, apply storytelling with data to influence decisions, and champion a culture of innovation and excellence.
Join us to build the future of risk services, where your expertise and curiosity will make a meaningful impact on our clients and our business. As a Risk Engineer, you’ll partner with clients and internal teams to deliver innovative risk solutions that protect people, property, and profitability. This is not just about assessing risk—it’s about shaping the future of risk management through expertise, technology, and proactive consulting.
We hire Risk Engineers across multiple specialty areas and experience levels. Your placement will be based on your background and level of expertise within specific lines (e.g., construction, energy, property, national accounts, inland marine) and/or multi-line. Field roles will work with all lines of business (WC, GL, Products, Property & Auto).
What You’ll Do
Conduct risk assessments, site surveys, and deliver actionable recommendations to reduce loss exposure with focus on customer-centricity and our service model to form partnerships while mitigating risk
Collaborate with underwriting, claims, and customers to develop tailored risk improvement strategies.
Working with our distribution partners to help differentiate The Hartford in the marketplace.
Leverage data analytics and digital tools to provide insights and predictive solutions.
Serve as a trusted advisor, educating clients on emerging risks and best practices.
Contribute to innovation initiatives that integrate AI and human expertise for precision and speed.
What We’re Looking For
Experience:
Background in risk engineering, safety, or related fields; specialty expertise or multi-line experience preferred.
Skills:
Technical knowledge in risk control and industry standards
Strong consultative skills to market risk mitigation solutions
Self-starters who can independently manage a client service book across a designated territory.
Technical writing proficiency to effectively communicate risk evaluation and mitigation measures to clients and underwriters
Ability to analyze data and tell a compelling story with insights
Proficiency with digital tools (dashboards, mobile apps, client platforms) and openness to emerging technologies
Customer-centric mindset and collaborative approach
Certification in safety or risk management disciplines (such as CSP, CIH, CPCU, CFPS, ARM) is a plus.
Education:
Degree in engineering, safety, or related discipline (or equivalent experience).
Why Join Us
Be part of a future-focused team integrating AI and human expertise.
Work on diverse projects across industries and geographies that align with your expertise
Access continuous learning and career development opportunities.
Make an impact by helping businesses thrive safely and sustainably.
Ready to Shape the Future of Risk Mitigation?
Apply today and explore how your expertise can make a difference.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$102,400 - $153,600
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

title: Director, Software Asset Management (SAM) Strategy and Operations
desc:
Director IT Engineering - IE06AE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Director of SAM Strategy & Operations, will execute on our vision of a fully mature SAM practice with complete license transparency, no audit surprises, continuous compliance, and optimized software spend by owning the SAM strategy and roadmap, driving cross-functional governance, and ensuring full utilization of our ServiceNow SAM Pro platform to deliver measurable results.
Reporting to the AVP of IT Engineering, the Director will lead a team of SAM analysts and an Open-Source Software (OSS) compliance analyst. This external-facing role is pivotal in elevating The Hartford’s SAM maturity – from establishing robust license management processes to championing audit readiness and cost optimization initiatives – all while collaborating across IT, Finance, Procurement, and Security to embed SAM excellence into the organization’s DNA.
This role will have a
Hybrid work schedule,
with the expectation of working in an office (Hartford, CT, Charlotte, NC, Columbus, OH or Chicago, IL) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Must be eligible to work in the US without company sponsorship.
Key Responsibilities:
Strategic Leadership & Roadmap Ownership:
Define and execute the enterprise-wide SAM vision and multi-year roadmap, aligning SAM objectives with The Hartford’s business goals and ITAM maturity targets. Drive program initiatives across discovery, data quality, process integration, and automation to achieve a “Managed/Optimized” SAM state.
Governance & Cross-Functional Collaboration:
Chair the SAM steering committee and governance boards, enforcing SAM policies and accountability across business units. Partner with federated teams – including Finance, Procurement, Security, and IT – to ensure software asset policies are adopted, roles (RACI) are clear, and compliance is everyone’s responsibility.
Audit Readiness & Compliance Assurance:
Ensure The Hartford is always audit-ready. Oversee license compliance audits and internal reviews, proactively resolving any compliance gaps to eliminate unplanned true-up costs or penalties. Establish continuous self-audit processes and metrics.
ServiceNow SAM Pro Optimization:
Maximize the use of ServiceNow SAM Pro across its full capabilities. Oversee SAM Pro configuration, data normalization efforts, and integration with other systems (e.g. CMDB, procurement tools) to achieve a single source of truth for software assets. Drive automation of license tracking, reclamation, and dashboard reporting to reduce manual effort and improve responsiveness.
Cost Optimization & Value Delivery:
Identify and drive software cost-saving opportunities by analyzing license utilization and entitlements. Lead initiatives for license reclamation, recycling of unused licenses, and contract optimization negotiations, aiming to reduce software spend while maintaining service levels. Track and report realized savings to demonstrate SAM’s impact on the bottom line.
Team Leadership & Development:
Build, mentor, and direct SAM Analysts and OSS compliance staff, providing guidance on complex license analyses and compliance issues. Foster an outcomes-driven culture with clear objectives (OKRs) and develop the team’s expertise in SAM Pro and asset analytics.
Policy Development & Continuous Improvement:
Oversee the development and updating of SAM policies, standards, and processes in coordination with IT Asset Management (ITAM) governance team. Ensure alignment with relevant regulations (e.g. NYDFS 500.13 cybersecurity requirements) and internal audit controls. Champion continuous improvement – regularly assess the SAM program’s maturity, and sponsor initiatives (training, tool enhancements, process refinements) to advance to the next level.
Required Qualifications & Experience:
Education & Experience:
10+ years of experience in IT Asset Management/SAM, with 5+ years in a leadership role overseeing enterprise SAM or ITAM programs. Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Business, or related field; MBA or relevant master’s a plus.
Domain Expertise:
Deep knowledge of software licensing practices and SAM frameworks. Proven track record of managing software audits and compliance for large organizations, including successful audit defenses and cost recoveries. Familiarity with industry standards and regulations affecting asset management (license terms, NIST, NYDFS, etc.).
Technical Skills:
Expertise in ServiceNow SAM Pro (or similar SAM tools) – including implementing modules, configuring workflows, and leveraging the platform for license reconciliation and optimization. Strong understanding of IT infrastructure (servers, cloud, endpoints) and discovery/CMDB data as it relates to software asset tracking. Ability to interpret software license agreements across major vendors (Microsoft, Oracle, IBM, etc.).
Leadership & Collaboration:
Exceptional leadership skills to drive cross-functional initiatives and influence stakeholders at all levels. Experience leading governance efforts or steering committees is highly desirable. Able to secure executive sponsorship and promote a culture of compliance and asset stewardship across diverse teams.
Analytical & Strategic Mindset:
Data-driven approach to decision-making, with experience defining and tracking KPIs/OKRs. Capable of strategic thinking to align SAM operations with business objectives and of diving into details to solve complex licensing problems. Excellent communication skills to articulate vision and value of SAM to executives and front-line teams alike.
Innovation & Continuous Improvement:
Demonstrated ability to implement process improvements and automation in asset management. Experience with integration of SAM tools with procurement systems, financial systems, or CI/CD pipelines is a plus. Passion for staying current on SAM industry trends, emerging tools, and best practices.
100% Policy & Governance Adoption:
Achieve full participation in SAM governance processes (steering committees, compliance reporting) across all business units, evidenced by 100% of top software asset owners completing regular compliance attestations and policy adherence checks.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$153,600 - $230,400
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

Maximize the use of ServiceNow SAM Pro across its full capabilities. Oversee SAM Pro configuration, data normalization efforts, and integration with other systems (e.g. CMDB, procurement tools) to achieve a single source of truth for software assets. Drive automation of license tracking, reclamation, and dashboard reporting to reduce manual effort and improve responsiveness.
Cost Optimization & Value Delivery:
Identify and drive software cost-saving opportunities by analyzing license utilization and entitlements. Lead initiatives for license reclamation, recycling of unused licenses, and contract optimization negotiations, aiming to reduce software spend while maintaining service levels. Track and report realized savings to demonstrate SAM’s impact on the bottom line.
Team Leadership & Development:
Build, mentor, and direct SAM Analysts and OSS compliance staff, providing guidance on complex license analyses and compliance issues. Foster an outcomes-driven culture with clear objectives (OKRs) and develop the team’s expertise in SAM Pro and asset analytics.
Policy Development & Continuous Improvement:
Oversee the development and updating of SAM policies, standards, and processes in coordination with IT Asset Management (ITAM) governance team. Ensure alignment with relevant regulations (e.g. NYDFS 500.13 cybersecurity requirements) and internal audit controls. Champion continuous improvement – regularly assess the SAM program’s maturity, and sponsor initiatives (training, tool enhancements, process refinements) to advance to the next level.
Required Qualifications & Experience:
Education & Experience:
10+ years of experience in IT Asset Management/SAM, with 5+ years in a leadership role overseeing enterprise SAM or ITAM programs. Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Business, or related field; MBA or relevant master’s a plus.
Domain Expertise:
Deep knowledge of software licensing practices and SAM frameworks. Proven track record of managing software audits and compliance for large organizations, including successful audit defenses and cost recoveries. Familiarity with industry standards and regulations affecting asset management (license terms, NIST, NYDFS, etc.).
Technical Skills:
Expertise in ServiceNow SAM Pro (or similar SAM tools) – including implementing modules, configuring workflows, and leveraging the platform for license reconciliation and optimization. Strong understanding of IT infrastructure (servers, cloud, endpoints) and discovery/CMDB data as it relates to software asset tracking. Ability to interpret software license agreements across major vendors (Microsoft, Oracle, IBM, etc.).
Leadership & Collaboration:
Exceptional leadership skills to drive cross-functional initiatives and influence stakeholders at all levels. Experience leading governance efforts or steering committees is highly desirable. Able to secure executive sponsorship and promote a culture of compliance and asset stewardship across diverse teams.
Analytical & Strategic Mindset:
Data-driven approach to decision-making, with experience defining and tracking KPIs/OKRs. Capable of strategic thinking to align SAM operations with business objectives and of diving into details to solve complex licensing problems. Excellent communication skills to articulate vision and value of SAM to executives and front-line teams alike.
Innovation & Continuous Improvement:
Demonstrated ability to implement process improvements and automation in asset management. Experience with integration of SAM tools with procurement systems, financial systems, or CI/CD pipelines is a plus. Passion for staying current on SAM industry trends, emerging tools, and best practices.
100% Policy & Governance Adoption:
Achieve full participation in SAM governance processes (steering committees, compliance reporting) across all business units, evidenced by 100% of top software asset owners completing regular compliance attestations and policy adherence checks.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. $153,600 - $230,400
