title: Risk Engineer (All Levels & Specialty Areas)
desc:
Sr Consultant Risk Engineering - KR08DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
At The Hartford, we are seeking top talent to meet our future needs - forward-thinking Risk Engineers who blend deep technical expertise with a passion for innovation, customer centricity, and digital fluency. In this risk mitigation role, you’ll leverage AI-powered tools, data-driven insights, and emerging technologies to deliver proactive, tailored risk solutions that help clients protect their assets and achieve their goals.
As a Risk Engineer, you’ll conduct survey work while working as a strategic advisor—collaborating across disciplines, driving digital transformation, and continuously learning to stay ahead of industry trends. You’ll use empathy and active listening to understand client priorities, apply storytelling with data to influence decisions, and champion a culture of innovation and excellence.
Join us to build the future of risk services, where your expertise and curiosity will make a meaningful impact on our clients and our business. As a Risk Engineer, you’ll partner with clients and internal teams to deliver innovative risk solutions that protect people, property, and profitability. This is not just about assessing risk—it’s about shaping the future of risk management through expertise, technology, and proactive consulting.
We hire Risk Engineers across multiple specialty areas and experience levels. Your placement will be based on your background and level of expertise within specific lines (e.g., construction, energy, property, national accounts, inland marine) and/or multi-line. Field roles will work with all lines of business (WC, GL, Products, Property & Auto).
What You’ll Do
Conduct risk assessments, site surveys, and deliver actionable recommendations to reduce loss exposure with focus on customer-centricity and our service model to form partnerships while mitigating risk
Collaborate with underwriting, claims, and customers to develop tailored risk improvement strategies.
Working with our distribution partners to help differentiate The Hartford in the marketplace.
Leverage data analytics and digital tools to provide insights and predictive solutions.
Serve as a trusted advisor, educating clients on emerging risks and best practices.
Contribute to innovation initiatives that integrate AI and human expertise for precision and speed.
What We’re Looking For
Experience:
Background in risk engineering, safety, or related fields; specialty expertise or multi-line experience preferred.
Skills:
Technical knowledge in risk control and industry standards
Strong consultative skills to market risk mitigation solutions
Self-starters who can independently manage a client service book across a designated territory.
Technical writing proficiency to effectively communicate risk evaluation and mitigation measures to clients and underwriters
Ability to analyze data and tell a compelling story with insights
Proficiency with digital tools (dashboards, mobile apps, client platforms) and openness to emerging technologies
Customer-centric mindset and collaborative approach
Certification in safety or risk management disciplines (such as CSP, CIH, CPCU, CFPS, ARM) is a plus.
Education:
Degree in engineering, safety, or related discipline (or equivalent experience).
Why Join Us
Be part of a future-focused team integrating AI and human expertise.
Work on diverse projects across industries and geographies that align with your expertise
Access continuous learning and career development opportunities.
Make an impact by helping businesses thrive safely and sustainably.
Ready to Shape the Future of Risk Mitigation?
Apply today and explore how your expertise can make a difference.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$102,400 - $153,600
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

title: Technical Lead and Product Owner – Snowflake Platform
desc:
Sr Cloud Engineer - IE07NE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Snowflake Technical Lead and Product Owner is a hands-on engineering and product lead role that provides technical leadership and mentoring for other engineers, and partners closely on solutions with Architecture, Principal Data Engineers (PDEs), Reliability Engineering (RE), Cloud Engineering, Security, and vendor teams to enable broader Snowflake capabilities, including supporting AI workloads and data products and ensuring platform capabilities continually improve and align with enterprise data strategies.
This role designs and automates the platform’s day-to-day operations, environments, CI/CD, observability, access, networking, and data movement so data engineering teams can ship governed, performant data products with confidence.
This role is accountable for defining and delivering the vision, roadmap, and value of The Hartford’s enterprise Snowflake data platform, combining strong technical expertise with modern product ownership to ensure the Snowflake environment is scalable, secure, reliable, cost efficient and easy to use for data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders.
This is a technical product role for an engineer with strong product instincts.  The role does not formally manage people and is not accountable for performance reviews.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations
will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Responsibilities
Platform Execution & Lifecycle Management
Provide mentoring, technical leadership and oversight for the Snowflake admin team (11 members), creating a collaborative environment and ensuring high‑quality delivery and alignment to the platform roadmap.
Lead platform rollout and adoption.
Evaluate new Snowflake features and enhancements.
Coordinate cross‑platform integrations, proofs of concept (PoCs), migration, and modernization initiatives (e.g. Cortex, hybrid tables, etc.).
Oversee Snowflake object administration activities (warehouses, databases, schemas, stages, roles) across the enterprise.
Partner with enterprise technology teams, including security, governance, audit, infrastructure, ensuring secure, governed access to the platform.
Set standards and guidelines related to engineering patterns, conventions and governance.
Support software as a service (SaaS) upgrade preparation and ensure clear communication to users.
Provide platform subject matter expertise (SME) guidance to engineering and RE teams.
Partner with enterprise AI teams to enable Cortex feature engineering at scale.
Reliability Engineering
Perform and/or partner with RE teams on the following activities:
Operational Stability: Incident, problem, availability management, disaster recovery (DR), cyber, patching, technical currency.
Automation and Delivery: Infrastructure as code (IaC), Continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD), automation, Terraform/GitHub actions (GHA) pipelines, observability dashboards, alerts, logging.
Compliance and Security: Vulnerability management, secrets, certificates, governance.
Product Ownership & Roadmap
Develop and maintain the Snowflake platform roadmap aligned to enterprise priorities.
Prioritize features, enhancements and technical requirements based on business value.
Manage platform intake, backlog grooming, and iteration planning.
User Enablement & Experience
Own user onboarding, documentation, training, and platform communications.
Lead the Snowflake user Community of Practice and drive platform best practices.
Partner with users to resolve usage challenges and support platform self‑service.
Manage the ServiceNow (MTE) service catalog for Snowflake offerings.
Vendor & Partner Management
Function as primary contact for Snowflake account and support teams.
Collaborate on contracts, licensing, quarterly business reviews (QBRs), and roadmap alignment.
Submit and track platform enhancement requests.
FinOps & Reporting
Deliver Snowflake platform usage, spend, and optimization reporting.
Recommend warehouse utilization and idle time changes to reduce cost without impairing performance SLAs.
Partner with vendor and internal teams to implement recommendations that optimize platform cost and performance.
Qualifications
Required
Candidates must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
5–7 years of engineering experience with modern data platforms.
3+ years of experience with day-to-day operation of a large-scale Snowflake implementation supporting development teams.
Strong understanding of Snowflake architecture, performance concepts, role-based access control (RBAC), and data pipelines.
Strong understanding of security, resiliency, DevOps, complex/large-scale data pipelines, observability, and related automation.
AI experience, specifically Snowflake Cortex and overall understanding of how Snowflake supports AI, agentic solutions.
Demonstrated ability to manage a roadmap, prioritize work, and collaborate with engineering teams.
Experience supporting user communities, documentation, training, and enablement.
Ability to communicate and influence technology across technical and business teams.
Strategic thinking and ability to simplify complex topics.
Preferred
SnowPro Core or SnowPro Advanced certification.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Google, Azure) and DevOps tooling (Terraform, GitHub).
Exposure to regulated industry environments (insurance, financial services).
Success Measures
Clear, actionable Snowflake platform roadmap established.
Consistent and scalable user onboarding, documentation, and support model.
Improved platform usability, performance, and cost efficiency.
Strong operating model between Product Management and Reliability Engineering.
Delivering high‑impact platform capabilities aligned with enterprise data strategy.
Positive user satisfaction feedback across engineering and business communities.
Compensation

Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. title: AVP, Head of Underwriting & Shared Services
desc:
AVP Underwriting - UO05AE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Summary
Reporting to the VP, Head of Group Reinsurance Plus, this is a strategic, high-visibility leadership role shaping the future of GRP Underwriting.
The
Assistant Vice President, Head of Underwriting & Shared Services
is a senior leadership role in Group Reinsurance Plus (GRP) accountable for setting the strategic direction, performance standards, and operational excellence of GRP’s Underwriting and Shared Services organization. This leader oversees teams responsible for underwriting the full GRP product portfolio including Life, Disability and Supplemental Health products and ensures disciplined risk management, profitable growth, and delivery of best-in-class underwriting services to GRP’s reinsurance clients.
As a key member of the GRP leadership team, the AVP sets the tone for culture, engagement, talent development, and execution excellence across the Underwriting and Shared Services functions.   For a leader ready to expand their scope, drive transformation, and help define what’s next for GRP, this is a chance to step into a role where your contributions will be seen, valued, and felt across the business.
About Group Reinsurance Plus
: GRP
helps insurance carriers grow their business in the group benefits marketplace with our market leading private label products & services and reinsurance risk protection solutions.  Our private label services include product development, claims, actuarial, underwriting, policy issuance and marketing support.
Core Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership & Business Performance
Establish and execute the underwriting and shared services vision and strategy for GRP.
Deliver GRP operating plan commitments tied to premium, sales, persistency, pricing and expense performance.
Monitor underwriting results and GRP scorecard metrics; proactively identify gaps and drive corrective actions through analytics and predictive modeling.
Represent GRP with intermediaries and clients to elevate GRP brand reputation and strengthen strategic partnerships through development of win-win partnership solutions.
Build and execute Underwriting & Shared Services strategic business plan with clear objectives, timelines and accountabilities to ensure delivery of commitments.
Underwriting Excellence & Governance
Oversee presale, new business, and renewal underwriting to ensure compliance with guidelines, authority limits, and pricing protocols.
Provide executive level guidance on complex risk decisions, pricing strategy, and block management across all product lines.
Quality, Best Practices and Learning
Oversee the quality management (QM) program to elevate consistency, accuracy and underwriting discipline.
Advance underwriting best practices and knowledge management initiatives that enhance speed, transparency and decision quality.
Oversee client and underwriting learning education programs that build technical expertise, ensure consistent application of the guidelines and support underwriter readiness at all levels.
Transformation, Innovation & Efficiency
Incorporate emerging trends, market intelligence, and customer insights to drive underwriting, product and process innovation.
Lead or sponsor strategic transformation initiatives to modernize underwriting programs, advance business intelligence, simplify processes, and enhance client experience.
Partner with GRP SLT to shape market positioning, growth initiatives, and competitive differentiation.
Build strong relationships with Employee Benefits underwriting and shared services leaders to leverage enterprise knowledge and innovation to advance GRP capabilities and expertise.
Leadership, Culture & Talent Development
Build and lead a high-performing, future-ready organization through workforce planning, organizational design, talent development, and succession planning.
Inspire teams with clear vision, transparent communication, and a commitment to operational and cultural excellence
Ensure strong operating routines are in place that maximize collaborraion, productivity, engagement and achievement of performance goals.
​
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree or professional designation preferred.
10+ years of underwriting experience in group life and disability products; reinsurance experience strongly preferred.
Proven leadership in managing underwriting teams and cross-functional business initiatives.
Demonstrated executive presence and influencing stakeholders at all levels.
Expertise in underwriting governance, pricing protocols, and quality management.
Exceptional presentation, communication, collaboration, negotiation, change management and relationship management skills.
Willingness to travel as required (up to 20%)
Position can be filled Hybrid or Remote.

Reporting to the VP, Head of Group Reinsurance Plus, this is a strategic, high-visibility leadership role shaping the future of GRP Underwriting.
The
Assistant Vice President, Head of Underwriting & Shared Services
is a senior leadership role in Group Reinsurance Plus (GRP) accountable for setting the strategic direction, performance standards, and operational excellence of GRP’s Underwriting and Shared Services organization. This leader oversees teams responsible for underwriting the full GRP product portfolio including Life, Disability and Supplemental Health products and ensures disciplined risk management, profitable growth, and delivery of best-in-class underwriting services to GRP’s reinsurance clients.
As a key member of the GRP leadership team, the AVP sets the tone for culture, engagement, talent development, and execution excellence across the Underwriting and Shared Services functions.   For a leader ready to expand their scope, drive transformation, and help define what’s next for GRP, this is a chance to step into a role where your contributions will be seen, valued, and felt across the business.
About Group Reinsurance Plus
: GRP
helps insurance carriers grow their business in the group benefits marketplace with our market leading private label products & services and reinsurance risk protection solutions.  Our private label services include product development, claims, actuarial, underwriting, policy issuance and marketing support.

title: Private and Non-Profit Management Liability Underwriter
desc:
Executive Underwriter - UW07CD
Senior Underwriter - UW08CA
Underwriter - UW08CB
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
We’re in the business of helping people achieve amazing things by protecting the things that matter most. And you can help us when you join our team of underwriters. They’re at the center of everything we do – and by joining us, you’ll be on a team known throughout the industry for providing our customers with just the right amount of coverage they need to pursue their unique interests.
Your underwriting career here means collaboration, inclusivity, innovative thinking and so much more. Here, doing things the way they’ve always been done isn’t a part of the job. Here, you’ll be putting your creative insights to work with other underwriters and leaders, having a seat at the table with key decision makers. And you’ll have any number of opportunities for your career to grow in whatever direction you choose.
The Private and Not-For-Profit Management Liability Underwriter is accountable for executing underwriting strategy while balancing profit, pricing and production objectives within a team environment. Strong sales behaviors and strong technical expertise are key factors in driving the desired results of the territory operating plan. The Underwriter is responsible for profitably growing our private company and not-for-profit management liability book within our field organization, providing industry leading customer value and demonstrating a ‘strive for excellence’ environment.
We’ll Be a Good Match if You Have:
A customer-first mindset, putting our customers at the center of everything you do.
A passion for making decisions through both analyzing the data and employing critical thinking skills.
A team spirit and desire to work collaboratively.
A financial mindset to help make the best decisions.
Ability to own your work and following through on commitments.
Ability to decipher and execute within a fluid and changing business environment.
An understanding of how to build relationships and trust among diverse groups.
The ability to advance their careers into technical OR leadership positions
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s Degree preferred, or equivalent combination of education, training and experience
2 + years of Management Liability underwriting experience
Demonstrated success in developing and maintaining solid relationships with all internal and external business partners
Excellent communication, interpersonal and presentation skills
An ability to think analytically about business problems, make recommendations and propose solutions.
High energy self-starter, who is resilient and has an entrepreneurial spirit
Demonstration of solid time, organizational, and desk management skills
Goal-oriented and delivers outcomes
Ability to challenge the status quo and compete to win
Superior technical knowledge and sound decision-making and analytical skills.
Valid driver’s license, as company pool car may be provided
***Job title and level will be determined after careful consideration of the selected candidate’s qualifications***
Location:
This role will have a Hybrid work arrangement, with the expectation of working in Chicago, IL office or Bloomington, MN office three days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).

title: Senior Staff Attorney Insurance Defense
desc:
Sr Staff Attorney - LM07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford currently has an in-house remote opportunity for a Senior Staff Attorney to litigate cases throughout the New York City area. This position is an ideal fit for an Attorney with litigation experience in premises actions, motor-vehicle accidents, property damage claims and No-Fault claims who wishes to practice in a collegial firm with other experienced litigators. The Senior Staff Attorney will strive to deliver the best possible results in pending litigation. In this position, the attorney reports to the Staff Legal Managing Attorney and the Assistant Managing Attorney. Our selected candidate shall demonstrate the ability to independently handle cases.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Independently handle lawsuits involving premises actions, motor-vehicle accidents, property damage claims and No-Fault claims.
Prepare pleadings, written discovery, reports, motions and briefs in support of defense strategies without supervision.
Independently prepare and present witnesses and evidence at depositions, trials, judicial and administrative hearings, arbitrations and alternative dispute resolutions.
Research, draft, file and argue appellate briefs in reviewing courts on behalf of clients.
Conduct legal research and independently develop and present defense strategies on behalf of clients.
Analyze substantive and procedural legal and factual issues.
Provide effective and timely communications, legal advice and other services to clients and claims customers on legal and factual issues in a technology driven environment.
Communicate with the court, witnesses, opposing counsel and co-counsel in a manner consistent with established office procedures.
Prompt, efficient and effective disposition of assigned cases.
Attend continuing legal education seminars in order to maintain high level of professional expertise.
QUALIFICATIONS:
Minimum of five (5) years of legal litigation experience.
Juris Doctorate (JD) from an accredited law school and license to practice in New York State.
Knowledge of New York Supreme and Civil courts.
Member in good standing of New York State Bar.
Federal admission in S.D.N.Y and E.D.N.Y is a plus.
Strong legal research and writing skills.
Excellent organizational skills and ability to prioritize duties and time are essential.
Strong communication, computer and technological skills, and the ability to quickly leverage new software are required.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. title: Sales Executive - Atlanta/GA/Mississippi/Alabama market
desc:
Sales Executive - SG08DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford's U100 Group Benefits Sales Team is seeing a Sales Executive for our Atlanta/GA/Alabama/Mississippi market.
The
Sales Executive
will work with independent producers and brokers to develop strong relationships and profitable business within U100 employee segments of the group benefits market. As The Hartford is looking to grow our Group Benefits small employer business segment, we're continuing to make investments in our U100 Segment and adding dedicated U100 Sales Executives. U100 Sales Executives will operate in a hybrid environment using a full suite of tools with agency partners.
The Sales Executive will be responsible for the building and execution of a Territory Sales Plan which aggressively grows customer base and achieves profit targets for The Hartford’s group life, group short-term disability, group long term disability and supplemental health lines of business.
*Must be within Atlanta/GA/Mississippi/Alabama market to be considered*
This will be accomplished through:
Conducting high volumes of quality sales calls with targeted production partners and new prospects
Establishing a sales call continuum where sales topics are generated from prior sales calls and executed with a set agenda designed to achieve specific outcomes
Creating demand and excitement for The Hartford products and services offered for small businesses
Educating partner producers and brokers on The Hartford group benefits small business products and services
Maintaining a disciplined operating routine and sales process designed to ensure consistent execution of highly effective sales practices
Developing an acute understanding of an assigned territory, including identifying a hierarchy of targeted producers and leveraging those relationships to attain territory financial goals.
Understanding the group benefits market landscape and competitive positioning to create innovative ways to leverage The Hartford value proposition
Align The Hartford strategies with supporting initiatives, tools and resources to aggressively grow market share and in force premium of the Sales Executive’s assigned territory
Developing and maintaining an effective pipeline of business opportunities to quote
Establishing an adequate day-to-day flow of cases to meet company and agency growth objectives
Effectively managing multiple initiatives, projects, and tasks focused on creating desired outcomes in the territory.
Qualifications:
2-5 years of experience in the insurance industry desired. Preferably with group benefits or agency experience.
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and experience strongly preferred
Exceptional sales and negotiation skills, preferably in a business-to-business capacity
Outside sales experience or demonstrated ability to be a high performer in market
Proven track record of developing strong business relationships
Ability to influence decision making with key partners and closing skills
Advanced insurance market and product knowledge
Strong interpersonal skills and customer focus
Demonstrates a continuing commitment to personal development (e.g. enrollment/participation in industry associations, relevant industry designations such as CIC, CPCU, LUTCF, GBDS, ARM; or College programs)
Top candidates will be flexible, innovative & willing to test new approaches
Strong enthusiasm and ability to work in a fast-growing environment
Superior verbal and written communication skills
Highly motivated self- starter with strong organizational capabilities
Certifications/Licenses
Accident, Health & Life Insurance License (within first six months in role)
This role is eligible for a competitive base salary of $60,000 plus an incentive compensation plan.
This role follows a Hybrid Work Arrangement that includes time spent traveling within your market, working remotely, and collaborating in‑office. You can expect to work three days per week (Tuesday through Thursday) in one of our office locations, with additional flexibility to work remotely when not in the field.
This structure provides the balance you need while ensuring meaningful in‑person engagement with peers, leaders, and partners. Time in‑market—meeting with producers, visiting agencies, and supporting field activities—is also a key component of development. Altogether, this model supports strong collaboration, relationship-building, and hands‑on learning as you grow your career with The Hartford.
As a condition of your employment, you must obtain and maintain the Accident, Health & Life license.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$60,000 - $60,000
Equal Opportunity

title: Business Data Consultant - Hybrid
desc:
Consultant Bus Data Analysis - GA08BE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Actuarial Information Services department’s Data Analysis Support team is looking for a skilled Sr. Business Data Consultant to join us. The ideal candidate will possess strong regulatory reporting knowledge and demonstrate excellent analytical and research abilities. Our team delivers statistical data from all P&C segments to both internal and external business customers. We support Enterprise Wide and Actuarial initiatives by providing expertise in data knowledge and statistical data capturing. This role is essential for ensuring timely and high-quality data reporting.
To excel in this role, you will need exceptional communication skills, experience in data analysis, and strong SQL abilities to gather required statistical data from various data sources. You will engage with a diverse array of business customers, including external regulators, internal compliance teams, finance departments, and IT partners across the enterprise.
This role will have a Hybrid work schedule, with the expectation of working in an office location (Hartford, CT) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Responsibilities:
Oversee the entire process of data reporting, ensuring timely and effective communication with all stakeholders.
Conduct data analysis and profiling on premium and loss statistics
Work together with internal partners determine needs, gather requirements, and develop solutions for data assets and reporting
Collaborate with our technical team to guarantee that all provided statistical data meets or surpasses customer expectations
Offer support and consulting by addressing inquiries, investigating solutions to complex issues, and promoting open communication between multiple teams
Conduct root cause analysis on identified issues, providing assistance to resolve data quality problems.
Supports the upkeep of data mapping documents and other design artifacts that outline business requirements and transformation rules
Foster a positive work environment by exemplifying cultural values and encouraging others to promote collaboration, accountability, diversity, and continuous improvement
Qualifications:
Candidates must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
3+ years of experience in business requirements or data analysis supporting the Insurance industry and data warehouse or reporting/analytic projects
Strong critical thinking skills and analytical ability
Proficient in industry standard querying tools
Proven ability to organize and manage multiple priorities in a timeline driven environment
Advanced business acumen with technical aptitude
Forward looking and focused on continuous improvement
Detail oriented with strong communication skills (both verbal and non-verbal)
Strong Insurance Product Knowledge

title: Public Relations Coordinator
desc:
Digital Marketing Associate - 87MG7N
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Hartford Funds is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Public Relations Coordinator to support our growing PR function. This role is ideal for a self-starter early in their communications career who is eager to learn, take ownership quickly, and contribute to meaningful media relations efforts that elevate Hartford Funds’ visibility and reputation. Working closely with the Head of Public Relations, you will assist with proactive media outreach, research and strategy development, writing and content planning, reporting, and day-to-day operational support.
Key Responsibilities
Monitor market trends, news cycles, peer activity, and new media opportunities to help shape PR strategy
Support proactive media relations efforts, including identifying relevant reporters/outlets, maintaining media lists, and drafting and distributing media pitches
Maintain media relationships, manage logistics, and help prepare subject matter experts for interviews
Draft, edit, and/or proofread PR materials such as press releases, briefing documents, key messages, talking points, and other external communications
Monitor for media coverage and support amplification across internal and external channels
Manage monthly reporting and maintain tracking systems to ensure operational efficiency
Assist with planning and execution of PR campaigns and company announcements
Contribute to issues management as needed, including research, monitoring, and material development
Support other brand-building and reputation-enhancing initiatives through research, project management, and material development as needed
Perform administrative tasks related to PR/marketing operations and team coordination
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Public Relations, Communications, Journalism, or a related field
1-2 years of relevant experience in public relations, communications, media, marketing, or a related field; financial services industry experience a plus
Familiarity with media relations concepts and an interest in pitching and reporter engagement
Interest in financial markets, investing, personal finance, and/or related topics a plus
Proficiency in Microsoft Office
Key Competencies
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate clearly and professionally
Attention to detail and commitment to accuracy, quality, and adherence to compliance standards
Media savvy with an understanding of the news cycle
Strong time-management skills with the ability to balance multiple deadlines
Interpersonal skills and ability to work collaboratively with internal and external partners
Compensation

title: Sales Manager - Southeast Region
desc:
GB Sales Manager - SM07GE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Regional Sales Manager to lead our U100 Sales Team across the Southeast Region. This sales leadership role is responsible for managing a team of in‑market Sales Executives, driving sales performance, and fostering a high‑performance culture. The ideal candidate will bring deep producer‑facing experience, strong coaching capabilities, and a passion for developing talent to exceed growth objectives.
Key Responsibilities
Sales Leadership (50%)
Directly manage a team of Sales Executives across the Southeast Regional Sales Offices, including
Atlanta
and
Tampa
.
Provide ongoing coaching, performance feedback, and development planning.
Conduct regular one‑on‑ones, ride‑alongs (virtual or in‑person), and pipeline reviews to ensure accountability and growth.
Foster a culture of collaboration, innovation, and results.
Market & Territory Influence (25%)
Apply strong knowledge of the Southeast EB market to identify opportunities, competitive dynamics, and influential producers across Atlanta, Tampa, and surrounding territories.
Partner effectively with key brokers, consultants, GA partners, and other distribution stakeholders to activate growth opportunities.
Leverage market insights — including competitive positioning and product trends — to accelerate new business momentum and elevate Hartford’s presence in priority markets.
Lead territory planning that optimizes visibility, influence, and production impact.
Monitoring Results & Feedback Loops (15%)
Collaborate with Employee Benefits (EB) teams to implement feedback loops centered on the agency experience.
Use feedback to inform future iterations of the agency offer and improve operational execution.
Strategic Initiatives & Market Development (10%)
Support execution of national and regional sales strategies, including dental and vision product expansion, API integration, and special agency programs.
Represent the Southeast Region in leadership forums and contribute to broader U100 initiatives.
Qualifications
3+ years of experience in front‑line sales management, preferably within
Employee Benefits
or
Group Insurance
, or equivalent work experience.
Strong understanding of agency distribution models and territory management.
Demonstrated success in coaching and developing sales talent.
Excellent communication, strategic thinking, and relationship‑building skills.
Ability to travel within the Southeast Region as needed.
Candidate must reside within the Southeast Region, preferably in
metro Atlanta
or
metro Tampa
.
Hybrid work arrangement: remote, in‑office, and field travel.
Compensation & Benefits
Competitive base salary and annual incentive plan aligned with the Sales Manager role.
Eligibility for referral bonus programs.
Comprehensive benefits package including health, retirement, and professional development support.
*Candidate must reside within the Southeast Region, preferably in metro Atlanta or metro Tampa*
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement. Candidates who live near one of our office locations will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).   Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$102,400 - $153,600

title: Senior Data Scientist
desc:
Sr Data Scientist - GD07AE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford’s Actuarial Strategic Modeling (ASM) organization is seeking a Senior Data Scientist to strengthen analytical and modeling capabilities supporting pricing, segmentation, underwriting strategies, and portfolio insights within Business insurance.
What You Will Do
Model Development & Analytics
• Contribute and lead key phases of end-to-end model development including problem definition, data exploration, model development, validation, and monitoring.
• Engage in the development and enhancement of predictive models — while thoughtfully assessing alternative approaches and techniques to support loss, territory/Geography, and emerging claim level modeling needs.
• Partner with Data Engineering and MLOps teams to support deployment and monitoring.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
• Partner with Actuarial, Underwriting, Product, Data Engineering, and Governance teams to align modeling work with business strategies.
• Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
Innovation & Modernization
• Explore new modeling methods, data sources, and tools.
• Contribute to shared modeling frameworks and best practices.
Technical Leadership & Mentorship
• Provide peer learning, code reviews, and technical guidance.
• Support continuous improvement of modeling standards.
Qualifications
Required
• 5+ years experience developing statistical or ML models.
• Master’s/PhD in Statistics, Data Science, Applied Math, Computer Science, Actuarial Science, or related field.
• Strong Python and SQL skills; experience with Git and cloud ML environments.
• Ability to collaborate across functions and communicate insights effectively.
Preferred
• Insurance analytics experience.
• Familiarity with model governance and monitoring.
• Experience with Agile delivery or MLOps pipelines.
• Ability to mentor and support the development of Data Science and Actuarial peers.
Why Join Us?
• Engage in high-visibility enterprise modeling initiatives.
• Work with modern analytical tools, ML, and generative AI.
• Work at the intersection of Data Science and Actuarial Science, partnering closely with diverse technical and business teams.
This role will have a Hybrid work schedule, with the expectation of working in an office (Columbus, OH, Chicago, IL, Hartford, CT or Charlotte, NC) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Candidates must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.

title: Underwriter Director-Latam
desc:
Managing Director - UQ06FB
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Navigators Re, a division of The Hartford, underwrites assumed treaty reinsurance totalling approximately $900 million in annual premiums across targeted lines of business worldwide. We are expanding and seeking talented, inquisitive colleagues to join our hard-working, respectful culture committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion.
This position reports to the SVP for Latin America and the Caribbean. It involves underwriting and technical pricing of surety treaties throughout the region. As a Senior Underwriter, you will interact extensively with reinsurance brokers and ceding companies. You will be responsible for all aspects of underwriting and pricing, as well as developing and nurturing relationships with our existing clients and new target companies.
This role includes expected business travel throughout the year including participation in the PASA General Assembly and Technical Seminar. The workload is particularly demanding during the months of May and June leading up to the July 1 renewals wherein approximately 60% of the portfolio renews as well as December wherein 20% of the portfolio renews at January 1.
Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship.  The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
Strong collaboration skills are essential for working effectively across functions and levels within the company. Experience in building relationships with brokers and clients is preferred. To satisfy these requirements, excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Spanish are required and while not required, Portuguese is viewed as a plus.
The candidate is expected to broaden and grown internally the company’s current LATAM surety portfolio.
WORK ARRANGEMENT:
This role will have a Hybrid work arrangement, with the expectation of working in an office (
Coral Gables
) 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). However, during peak renewal times/seasons, 5 days in the office could be required.
RESPONSIBILITES:
Support the Head of Latin America (LATAM) Surety underwriter in pricing new and renewal business and maintaining detailed knowledge of assigned clients and treaties.
Reviewing and approval of special acceptances which can include but not limited to performing financial analysis of a principle and submitting a formal proposal to the CUO for review and approval.
Excel in the use of the department’s pricing models.
Collaborate within a team framework to support the unit's overall goals.
Review treaty contracts governing our transactions.
Support the business's aggregation protocols.
Work with the Head of the Actuarial Team, the CUO and the Head of Latin America (LATAM) Surety to ensure that the company’s pricing tools are up to date.
When needed, work with the accounting and claims teams to ensure that our records / files are in order and that EPI management of the portfolio comports with the group’s protocols.
Support the CUO and the Head of Latin America (LATAM) Surety on various mentoring/training programs available for Jr. team members and new hires.
QUALIFICATIONS:
Over 8 years of demonstrated experience in surety reinsurance underwriting and pricing
Advanced Excel skills are preferred.
Proven ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Self-motivation, creativity, and strong skills in time management, project management, and customer management.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, capable of conveying technical concepts to diverse audiences in both English and Spanish is required.  The addition of Portuguese is a plus.
Flexibility and adaptability to changing priorities.
A process improvement mindset with a talent for developing efficiencies.
Ability to ready and interpret company financial statements is required.
Excellent technical, analytical, and data skills is preferred
Advanced Excel skills are essential.
Working knowledge in the use of SQL, Python, Tableau and Power BI is a plus
Strong financial background is a plus.
Bachelor’s Degree is required.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$185,920 - $278,880
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

title: Defined Contribution Consultant - Midwest / West Coast based
desc:
DC Partner Firm Specialist - 87SZ6E
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Position Summary: Serve as a defined contribution investment only (DCIO) expert, collaborating with retail Advisor Consultants and RIA Sales Consultants, to enhance Hartford Funds’ presence in the DCIO marketplace. Candidate will drive sales and revenue growth by building and managing strategic partnerships with key retirement business development teams at broker dealers and recordkeeping firms.
Key Responsibilities:
•Develop and execute business plans with internal field resources to service, prospect and meet sales goals within their territory.
•Build strong relationships with financial advisors, retirement consultants, and recordkeepers to support their business and provide them with product and market insights.
•Provide retirement professionals clarity on investment solutions, educate them on product offerings fit within retirement plan line-ups, and understand their needs for growing retirement plan business.
•Address complex inquiries and resolve issues for internal and external stakeholders.
•Develop sales strategies, manage a sales pipeline, and document all interactions in our CRM.
•Optimize time and travel budgets to maximize sales impact, including regular in-person client engagement.
Qualifications:
•Bachelor’s degree in business, economics, finance, sales, or marketing (or equivalent experience).
•3–5 years of sales experience in the DCIO or retirement marketplace preferred.
•Extensive knowledge of the defined contribution industry, including plan design, regulatory matters, investments, ERISA, distribution, and recordkeeping.
•Strong interpersonal, teamwork, and relationship-building skills.
•Excellent communication, presentation, and organizational abilities.
•Creative problem-solving skills and ability to provide guidance to the retail sales team.
•Self-motivated, able to work independently, and willing to travel overnight up to 50%.
•FINRA Series 7 and/or 63 required.

title: Workers Compensation - Business Consultant
desc:
Consultant Bus Data Analysis - GA08BE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
The Hartford is currently looking for a Workers Compensation Business Consultant to join the Regulatory Reporting Workers Compensation Financial Call Team. This role will be focused on providing business and data analysis support for several highly visible, business-critical applications.
The Workers Compensation Financial call team Provides information on Financial Call components, including Premiums, Losses, Claim Counts, and Loss Adjustment Expenses.
Provides information on what experience to include or exclude when reporting Financial Call data. The aggregate Financial Calls provide timely information needed for each state’s overall cost analysis to develop the state’s rate or loss cost filing needed by the rating Bureau’s.
Responsibilities:
Applies functional knowledge and techniques necessary to draw out data and analytics business requirements completely and accurately from stakeholders. Performs requirements planning, monitoring and requirements management.
Independently performs data analysis, data discovery and data profiling using industry standard querying tools to support business requirements development that are complete, clear, unambiguous, well written and appropriate to audience.
Maintains Data mapping documents, Business Matrix and other Data Design artifacts that define business data requirements and transformation rules.
Act as liaison and advisor/consultant between the business and IT stakeholders.
Performs and aid functional testing.
Structure and drive working and informational sessions that promote collaboration, synergy, and achieve desired outcomes.
Understands the scope of the project and helps resolve scope questions and issues related to own work.
Handle root cause analysis of business data issues and provides technical data guidance on issues.
Helps resolve scope of questions and issues.
Qualifications:
3+ years of experience in workers compensation and data analysis supporting the Insurance industry preferred.
Knowledge of Workers Compensation Insurance
Proficient in industry standard querying tools (Toad, Toad for Data Analysts, SQL, ect.)
Proficient in Excel, Building and updating macros.
Experience working on data warehouse or reporting/analytic projects.
Experience in industry standard querying tool
Experience working on Agile projects.
Strong facilitation, requirements elicitation and data analysis skills
Connected with industry best practices.
Proven ability to organize and manage multiple priorities in a timeline driven environment.
Presentation Skills
Focused on continuous improvements.
Interpersonal and influencing skills.
Effectively manages key stakeholders in a matrix environment.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work arrangement.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).   Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work arrangement, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise.

title: Y-Risk Emerging Technology, Executive Underwriter
desc:
Executive Underwriter - UW07BD
Senior Underwriter - UW08BA
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Y-Risk, as an underwriting company of The Hartford, partners with brokers to provide robust insurance solutions for our clients. We have proudly built a company operating on the leading edge of the insurance industry where we expertly merge the fundamentals of insurance with the fast-paced world of the tech-enabled innovation.
We are establishing an underwriting team in the Bay Area of California, in proximity to the San Francisco insurance community and the investment & technology resources in Silicon Valley.  We are building on the existing Y-Risk brand as an industry leader in
underwriting the innovators
: delivering client centric solutions as new business models emerge. This strategic move will align with Y-Risk's mandate of "insuring tomorrow's economy" by positioning the company at the forefront of underwriting emerging technologies. This team will leverage The Hartford’s deep institutional knowledge and resources through collaboration and transparency by focusing on the intersection of underwriting, data science, risk finance, risk engineering, technology, and claims.
Our team takes a nimble and creative approach to underwriting. We take the time to get to know and understand each client’s business and insurance goals and use their data and exposure profile to put the best possible coverage in place. We can appropriately price risk and make necessary adjustments based on active data analysis.
At Y-Risk, we also understand the need for speed. Quick turnaround in underwriting and structuring insurance programs is one of our top priorities.
In doing this, we value working closely with our customers and their brokers to communicate the benefits of our products.
We are seeking business line underwriters with expertise in strategic investments and a deep passion for emerging technologies.  Our Underwriters will monitor and respond to private equity investment and technological trends as they are being developed, while growing Y-Risk business and will report to the Managing Director.  This team will deliver underwriting solution expertise and 360⁰ perspectives on risk to clients and producers; and create forward-thinking insurance products that meet the needs of tomorrow's economy.
Responsibilities include:
Solution Expertise and Innovation:
Identify, target, and solicit specific emerging technologies and clients.
Seek opportunities to underwrite the business by direct solicitation to agents, brokers, and clients based on underwriting & risk analysis expertise.
Conduct exposure & pricing-based Research & Development – organizing and categorizing exposures to identify if new underwriting & pricing methodologies are required, or if “traditional” products, pricing, and appetite apply.
Financial & Strategic Investment:
Strategic planning and market analysis development, focused on investment trends and “following the money.”
Work closely with The Hartford’s deep institutional knowledge base and resources to approach and evaluate the businesses and technologies that will make up tomorrow’s economy.
Close collaboration with the Director of Partnerships & Ecosystems, housed in the Y-Risk Innovation Labs, and the Emerging Technology Study Group to:
actively engage with the local tech community
participate in technology related industry events
foster partnerships with leading tech companies
Underwriting:
Reviews, evaluates, quotes, binds & issues new business and assigned renewals.
Applies any applicable underwriting guidelines.
Properly documents customer file in accordance with regulatory and underwriting standards.
Establishes & maintains quality submission pipeline.
Manages book of business to greater than $5MM in premium within 24 months
Completes assigned projects per Project Tracker document.
Agency Management:
Maintains professional relationship provide excellent account management with assigned brokers.
Determines & executes strategy to increase quality submissions.
Works with brokers to positively influence individual submission/policy outcome.
Retains 90%+ of renewal book.
Travels as appropriate for book management & relationship building, (approximately once per month averaging 3-5 days per visit)
Qualifications:
3+ years of P&C carrier business lines underwriting experience is preferred; well versed in commercial Auto and GL a plus. Open to considering someone with P&C carrier/broker/agent expertise in the Sharing/Shared & On-Demand Economy industry.
Capable of underwriting highly complex business and will require limited support from senior management and infrequent referral activity to complete quotes, thorough knowledge of applicable laws, regulations, and governance.
Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical teams.
Innovative with ability to translate emerging technology opportunities to successfully underwrite business.
Superior technical knowledge and sound decision-making and analytical skills.
Executive presence and proven presentation abilities.
Stellar sales and negotiation skills.
Highly collaborative with strong relationship building skills.
Able to travel.
This role will have a Hybrid work schedule, with the expectation of working in the Downtown San Francisco office / in market 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$96,400 - $180,000
The posted salary range reflects our ability to hire at different position titles and levels depending on background and experience.
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

title: Lead AI Data Engineer - Hybrid
desc:
Sr Staff Data Engineer - GE07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Join our team as a Lead AI Data Engineer and lead the charge in developing cutting-edge AI solutions and data engineering strategies. Embrace our core values of innovation, collaboration, and excellence as you unlock unparalleled growth opportunities in the dynamic field of AI and data engineering. Shape the future of technology with us!  Apply now to be part of our innovative journey and make a significant impact!
Primary Job Responsibilities
AI Data Engineering lead responsible for Implementing AI data pipelines  that bring together structured, semi-structured and unstructured data to support AI and Agentic solutions. This Includes pre-processing with extraction, chunking, embedding and grounding strategies to get the data ready.
Develop AI-driven systems to improve data capabilities, ensuring compliance with industry best practices.
Implement efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures and integrate with enterprise data infrastructure.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate solutions into operational processes and systems supporting various functions.
Stay up to date with industry advancements in GenAI and apply modern technologies and methodologies to our systems.
Design, build and maintain scalable and robust real-time data streaming pipelines using technologies such as Apache Kafka, AWS Kinesis, Spark streaming, or similar.
Develop data domains and data products for various consumption archetypes including Reporting, Data Science, AI/ML, Analytics etc
Ensure the reliability, availability, and scalability of data pipelines and systems through effective monitoring, alerting, and incident management.
Implement best practices in reliability engineering, including redundancy, fault tolerance, and disaster recovery strategies.
Collaborate closely with DevOps and infrastructure teams to ensure seamless deployment, operation, and maintenance of data systems.
Mentor junior team members and engage in communities of practice to deliver high-quality data and AI solutions while promoting best practices, standards, and adoption of reusable patterns.
Develop graph database solution for complex data relationships supporting AI systems.
Apply GenAI solutions to insurance-specific data use cases and challenges.
Partner with architects and stakeholders to influence and implement the vision of the AI and data  pipelines while safeguarding the integrity and scalability of the environment.
Skills
Strong Technical Knowledge (AI solution leveraging Cloud and modern solutions)
Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical teams and influence
Collaboration across teams, decision making, conflict resolution and relationship building skills.
Experience in mentoring and developing Junior AI or Data Engineers.
Knowledge of evolving industry design patterns for AI.
Strong planning, organization, and execution skills.
Ability to provide Thought Leadership to dynamic and collaborative teams, demonstrating excellent interpersonal skills and time management capabilities.
Ability to understand and align deliverables to the departmental and organization strategies and objectives.
Ability to lead successfully in a lean, agile, and fast-paced organization, leveraging Scaled Agile principles and ways of working.
Leader and team player with a transformation mindset.
Ability to translate complex technical topics into business solutions and strategies, as well as turn business requirements into a technical solution.
Qualifications
Candidates must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position.
Bachelor's in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
8+ years of data engineering experience including Data solutions, SQL and NoSQL, Snowflake, ETL/ELT tools, CICD, Bigdata, Cloud Technologies (AWS/Google/AZURE), Python/Spark, Datamesh, Datalake or Data Fabric.
1+ years of data engineering experience focused on supporting Generative AI technologies
Strong hands-on experience implementing production ready enterprise grade GenAI data solutions.
Experience with prompt engineering techniques for large language models.
Experience in implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, integrating retrieval mechanisms with language models.
Experience of vector databases and graph databases, including implementation and optimization.
Experience in processing and leveraging unstructured data for GenAI applications.
Proficiency in implementing scalable AI driven data systems supporting agentic solution (AWS Lambda, S3, EC2, Langchain, Langgraph).
Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Experience with building AI pipelines that bring together structured, semi-structured and unstructured data.  This includes pre-processing with extraction, chunking, embedding and grounding strategies, semantic modeling, and getting the data ready for Models and Agentic solutions.
Experience in vector databases, graph databases, NoSQL, Document DBs, including design, implementation, and optimization. (e.g., AWS open search, GCP Vertex AI, Neo4j, Spanner Graph, Neptune, Mongo, DynamoDB etc.).
Experience in implementing data governance practices, including Data Quality, Lineage, Data Catalogue capture, holistically, strategically, and dynamically on a large-scale data platform.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).
Strong written and verbal communication skills and ability to explain technical concepts to various stakeholders.
Preferred Qualifications:
Experience in multi cloud hybrid AI solutions.
AI Certifications
Experience in P&C or Employee Benefits industry
Knowledge of natural language processing (NLP) and computer vision technologies.
Contributions to open-source AI projects or research publications in the field of Generative AI.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$135,040 - $202,560
Equal Opportunity
Employer/Sex/Race/Color/Veterans/Disability/Sexual
Orientation/Gender Identity or
Expression/Religion/Age

desc:
Sr Staff Data Engineer - GE07DE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
Join our team as a Lead AI Data Engineer and lead the charge in developing cutting-edge AI solutions and data engineering strategies. Embrace our core values of innovation, collaboration, and excellence as you unlock unparalleled growth opportunities in the dynamic field of AI and data engineering. Shape the future of technology with us!  Apply now to be part of our innovative journey and make a significant impact!
Primary Job Responsibilities
AI Data Engineering lead responsible for Implementing AI data pipelines  that bring together structured, semi-structured and unstructured data to support AI and Agentic solutions. This Includes pre-processing with extraction, chunking, embedding and grounding strategies to get the data ready.
Develop AI-driven systems to improve data capabilities, ensuring compliance with industry best practices.
Implement efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures and integrate with enterprise data infrastructure.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate solutions into operational processes and systems supporting various functions.
Stay up to date with industry advancements in GenAI and apply modern technologies and methodologies to our systems.
Design, build and maintain scalable and robust real-time data streaming pipelines using technologies such as Apache Kafka, AWS Kinesis, Spark streaming, or similar.
Develop data domains and data products for various consumption archetypes including Reporting, Data Science, AI/ML, Analytics etc
Ensure the reliability, availability, and scalability of data pipelines and systems through effective monitoring, alerting, and incident management.
Implement best practices in reliability engineering, including redundancy, fault tolerance, and disaster recovery strategies.
Collaborate closely with DevOps and infrastructure teams to ensure seamless deployment, operation, and maintenance of data systems.
Mentor junior team members and engage in communities of practice to deliver high-quality data and AI solutions while promoting best practices, standards, and adoption of reusable patterns.
Develop graph database solution for complex data relationships supporting AI systems.
Apply GenAI solutions to insurance-specific data use cases and challenges.
Partner with architects and stakeholders to influence and implement the vision of the AI and data  pipelines while safeguarding the integrity and scalability of the environment.
Skills
Strong Technical Knowledge (AI solution leveraging Cloud and modern solutions)
Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical teams and influence
Collaboration across teams, decision making, conflict resolution and relationship building skills.
Experience in mentoring and developing Junior AI or Data Engineers.
Knowledge of evolving industry design patterns for AI.
Strong planning, organization, and execution skills.
Ability to provide Thought Leadership to dynamic and collaborative teams, demonstrating excellent interpersonal skills and time management capabilities.
Ability to understand and align deliverables to the departmental and organization strategies and objectives.
Ability to lead successfully in a lean, agile, and fast-paced organization, leveraging Scaled Agile principles and ways of working.
Leader and team player with a transformation mindset.
Ability to translate complex technical topics into business solutions and strategies, as well as turn business requirements into a technical solution.

1+ years of data engineering experience focused on supporting Generative AI technologies

title: Technology and Cyber Technical Manager, Risk Engineering
desc:
Exec Tech Cons Risk Eng - KR07FE
We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the
future.
This position will be responsible for the development and deployment of Risk Engineering specialized practices for the Technology and Cyber Liability Line of Business, encompassing both cybersecurity and technology risk disciplines. This includes but is not limited to strategic planning, resource management, technical support, evaluation, and business development. The role may require future managerial duties for a staff of dedicated specialized Risk Engineering Consultants. Completion of field and virtual survey work on insured operations that demand the advanced technical skillsets will also be required. Identification of Risk Engineering recommendations, solutions, and opportunities for impactful service delivery will be a key skillset for this position.​
Our ideal candidate
:
A cybersecurity and technology risk leader with a passion for helping businesses reduce risk.
A strong communicator who can translate complex technical concepts into practical advice.
A mentor who enjoys developing others and building high-performing teams.
Someone who thrives in a collaborative, cross-functional environment.
Responsibilities:
The Manager Technology & Cyber Risk Engineering must drive the following business activities that support the needs of the organization.
Responsibilities may include:
Technical Acumen
– Demonstrated expertise in Technology Risk, Cyber Security, and related liability disciplines, with strong knowledge of core cyber and technology insurance coverages and perils. Responsible for managing the Cyber & Technology Liability line for all Risk Engineering services, including training, collateral development, and technical support for internal partners and external customers. Must maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of industry best practices in cybersecurity, IT resilience, cloud risk, and liability risk transfer.
Risk Engineering Staff Development
– Strengthen Technology and Cyber expertise across Risk Services by providing technical leadership and support to Risk Engineering Consultants. Serve as the subject matter expert through continued professional development and deliver training, mentoring, and technical guidance aligned with Underwriting needs. Assess, develop, and maintain technical content and resource materials. Support partners including Underwriting, Claims, Agents, and Insureds. Complete field surveys for the largest and most complex accounts when needed. Assist Underwriting sales initiatives, including attendance and support at sales presentations.
Segment Liaison
– Regularly communicate and collaborate with Cyber and Technology business partners and vendors to create a strong and credible relationship. Communicate information to Risk Services to ensure that they understand and act upon key initiatives and objectives.
Training
– Develop and implement key training initiatives for all products and services required by Technology and Cyber business partners. Enhance current state including technical manuals, training materials, and overall field support to develop and maintain technical information as required.  Work in conjunction with the Training Director to elevate training programs across disciplines.
Other (when applicable) i.e. Technical Resource, Special Projects, etc.
- Plan, organize, and manage resources to bring about the successful completion of specific project goals and objectives. Develop, implement, and evaluate project plan(s) for special projects as assigned. Monitor progress and take corrective action when necessary.
Qualifications:
Bachelor or Master degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Risk Management, or a related field.
10+ years of experience in cybersecurity and/or technology risk management, preferably within the insurance or financial services industry.
Professional certifications such as CISSP, CISM, CRISC, or CPCU with a cyber or technology risk focus.
Familiarity with insurance operations, policy administration systems, and regulatory compliance.
Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
Strong communication and consultative skills, with the ability to translate technical findings into actionable insights.
This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work schedule.  Candidates who live near one of our office locations will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday).Candidates who do not live near an office will have a remote work schedule, with the expectation of coming into an office as business needs arise. ​
Risk Assessment:
Must have a high degree of knowledge and skill to handle complex situations arising from new and renewal business submissions. Will work with large complex accounts to assess exposures and controls and provide underwriters and agents with direction to improve account conditions and lessen financial exposure.  Must identify new service opportunities or additional exposures to loss and mentor internal staff to do the same.
Risk Improvement Services:
Must have expertise to provide Risk Improvement service work, including operational loss analysis reports and investigations to direct services that eliminate or control conditions leading to potential losses. Ability to complete detailed service plans, provide consultative recommendations, and develop new tools and products as needed.  Must communicate service plan status in the context of The Hartford’s offerings to improve insureds’ control of exposures. Ability to manage complex multi-location accounts and support Field Operations.
Consultative Selling Skills:
Must demonstrate confidence and skill when working with insured accounts using a consultative approach. Based on their knowledge of the insureds business activities and needs, they can move the account in a direction which eliminates exposures and/or controls the potential for future financial loss.
Excellent Communication and Presentation Skills:
Must have high degree of knowledge and skill to make formal written and verbal presentations to our insured accounts and agents with the ability to influence and motivate others. Presentations must be well thought out and professionally constructed, as well as being clear, concise and focused. Reports to Management are expected to be of professional quality. These reports must provide direction for the insured, agent and underwriter to improve operations and reduce potential insurable loss.
Industry Specific Knowledge and Safety:
Must understand the Regional Office business plans and industry specific issues that affect each distinct territory. Have the confidence to speak about the issues that may affect the financial conditions of the region and offer advice to lessen our exposure. Provide support for Field staff in addressing these initiatives.
Business Tools:
Must be proficient in using Microsoft Office tools including Excel, PowerPoint and Word as well as presentation platforms such as GoToMeeting and Microsoft Teams.
Compensation
The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the external market. Actual base pay could vary and may be above or below the listed range based on factors including but not limited to performance, proficiency and demonstration of competencies required for the role. The base pay is just one component of The Hartford’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include short-term or annual bonuses, long-term incentives, and on-the-spot recognition. The annualized base pay range for this role is:
$130,400 - $195,600
Equal Opportunity
